title: Decide when cond is the right shape vs function heads
prompt:
Given:

```
def grade(score, extra_credit) do
  cond do
    score + extra_credit >= 90 -> :a
    score + extra_credit >= 80 -> :b
    score + extra_credit >= 70 -> :c
    score + extra_credit >= 60 -> :d
    true -> :f
  end
end
```

The computation `score + extra_credit` is repeated. Refactor to compute once, then use a pattern-matched helper: wrap in a private function `def grade(score, extra_credit), do: grade_from_total(score + extra_credit)` and then define `grade_from_total/1` as multi-clause function heads with guards. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Grader do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Requires seeing the repeated computation as a refactoring opportunity AND applying function heads correctly.
